category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Banking Supervision Awareness in Mauritius
items.description: Banks operating in Mauritius are licensed and supervised by the central bank, which sets prudential expectations around capital adequacy, governance, and conduct. Middleware that processes banking transactions should be designed so that supervisory expectations on accuracy, traceability, and resilience can be evidenced. Awareness of the supervisory perimeter helps teams anticipate where controls and reporting hooks are typically expected.
items.applicability: Used when scoping banking middleware features that touch licensed activities, so that design choices remain compatible with a supervised banking environment.
items.example: A payment-routing module records immutable processing metadata so that, if requested during a supervisory review, the institution can demonstrate how each transaction was handled end to end.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: AML/CFT Programme Awareness
items.description: Anti-money-laundering and counter-financing-of-terrorism obligations expect institutions to maintain a risk-based programme covering customer risk assessment, ongoing monitoring, and reporting. Middleware that touches customer or transaction data should make it possible to surface signals that feed an AML/CFT programme rather than obscure them. Designing for traceability supports the broader control environment expected of financial institutions.
items.applicability: Used when building or reviewing transaction-processing components that may need to expose data to downstream AML/CFT monitoring.
items.example: A transfer service emits structured event records (amount, counterparty reference, channel, timestamp) so a separate monitoring system can apply risk rules without re-parsing free text.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Awareness
items.description: Customer due diligence typically involves identifying and verifying customers, understanding the nature of the relationship, and refreshing information over time. Systems that capture identity attributes should treat them as controlled data with clear provenance and update history. Awareness of CDD themes helps developers avoid designs that make verification status ambiguous or unauditable.
items.applicability: Used when designing customer onboarding data models and the fields that record verification outcomes.
items.example: An onboarding record stores verification status, the evidence reference, the operator who completed it, and the timestamp, rather than a single free-text note.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Suspicious Transaction Escalation Awareness
items.description: When a transaction appears unusual or potentially linked to illicit activity, institutions are generally expected to escalate internally and, where warranted, report to the financial intelligence authority. Software should support escalation workflows without exposing the existence of a report to the customer (tipping-off concerns). Awareness of escalation themes informs how alert states and access controls are modelled.
items.applicability: Used when implementing alerting, case management, or escalation routing inside transaction-monitoring tooling.
items.example: An alert flagged as escalated becomes visible only to a restricted compliance role and is excluded from any customer-facing status surface.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Data Protection Awareness
items.description: Processing of personal data in Mauritius is governed by data-protection principles such as lawful purpose, data minimisation, accuracy, and security. Banking middleware routinely handles personal and financial data, so collection should be limited to what each function needs and access should be controlled. Awareness of these principles helps teams avoid over-collection and undocumented data flows.
items.applicability: Used when defining data models, retention behaviour, and access scopes for components that handle personal data.
items.example: A reporting view exposes only the fields required for the report and omits full identifier numbers unless a specific, justified need exists.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Account Opening Control Themes
items.description: Account opening typically combines identity verification, risk classification, and approval steps before an account becomes operational. Systems should make the path from application to activation explicit, with each gate recorded. Awareness of these control themes prevents designs where accounts can become active without the expected checks being captured.
items.applicability: Used when modelling account lifecycle states and the gating conditions that move an application toward an active account.
items.example: An account cannot transition to active until verification and risk-classification flags are both set and an approver action is recorded.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Payments and Transfers Processing Awareness
items.description: Payment and transfer processing depends on accurate beneficiary data, value-dating, and clear status transitions through initiation, processing, and completion or rejection. Middleware should treat each payment as a state machine with auditable transitions rather than a single mutable record. Awareness of these themes reduces the risk of silent value changes or ambiguous outcomes.
items.applicability: Used when implementing or reviewing payment initiation, status tracking, and rejection handling.
items.example: A payment record carries an explicit status (Initiated, Processing, Completed, Rejected) and each change is appended with actor and timestamp.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Direct Debit and Mandate Processing Awareness
items.description: Direct debit collection relies on a valid mandate that authorises a creditor to draw from a debtor's account within agreed parameters. Systems should bind each collection to a specific mandate and refuse collections where the mandate is missing, expired, or revoked. Awareness of mandate lifecycle helps avoid unauthorised or out-of-scope collections.
items.applicability: Used when implementing mandate storage and the validation that gates direct-debit collection runs.
items.example: A collection batch checks each item against an active mandate; items whose mandate is revoked are diverted to an exception queue rather than processed.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Settlement Processing Awareness
items.description: Settlement is the stage at which obligations between parties are discharged, and it must be handled with finality and integrity once confirmed. Middleware should distinguish provisional states from settled states and avoid reversing a settled position without an explicit, recorded correction. Awareness of settlement finality concepts prevents accidental double-processing or silent reversals.
items.applicability: Used when designing the boundary between in-flight transactions and settled positions.
items.example: Once a transaction is marked settled, any correction is recorded as a new compensating entry rather than an in-place edit of the settled record.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Reconciliation Control Awareness
items.description: Reconciliation compares independent records to confirm that balances and movements agree, and surfaces breaks for investigation. Software should make reconciliation breaks visible and traceable rather than silently absorbing differences. Awareness of reconciliation discipline supports the integrity expectations placed on financial systems.
items.applicability: Used when building reconciliation routines or interfaces that compare internal and external ledgers.
items.example: A reconciliation run produces a break list with the two values, the difference, and an aging indicator, and flags unresolved breaks beyond a threshold.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Lending Process Control Awareness
items.description: Lending decisions typically pass through application, assessment, approval, and disbursement stages with defined authority limits. Systems should record the basis of a decision and the approving authority so that the credit process is reconstructable. Awareness of lending control themes helps prevent disbursement without the expected approval evidence.
items.applicability: Used when modelling loan origination workflows and approval-authority enforcement.
items.example: A loan above a configured limit requires a higher approval tier, and disbursement is blocked until the matching approval is recorded.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Leasing Operations Awareness
items.description: Leasing arrangements involve an asset, a schedule of payments, and terms governing the asset over the lease period. Systems should track the asset linkage, the payment schedule, and the lease status as distinct, auditable elements. Awareness of leasing structures helps ensure obligations and asset status remain consistent over time.
items.applicability: Used when modelling lease contracts, payment schedules, and asset-linkage data.
items.example: A lease record links to its underlying asset and a schedule, and the system flags a mismatch if a payment is recorded against a terminated lease.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Credit Monitoring Awareness
items.description: Ongoing credit monitoring tracks the performance of exposures and the early signals of deteriorating repayment behaviour. Systems should make repayment status and trend information available so that monitoring is timely rather than retrospective. Awareness of monitoring themes supports designs that surface emerging risk before it becomes loss.
items.applicability: Used when building dashboards or feeds that report on the health of a credit portfolio.
items.example: A monitoring view highlights accounts whose payment pattern has changed (for example, a first missed instalment) so they can be reviewed promptly.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Arrears Management Awareness
items.description: Arrears arise when scheduled payments are missed, and they are typically tracked by aging buckets that drive collection actions. Systems should compute aging consistently and trigger the appropriate stage without manual ambiguity. Awareness of arrears handling helps ensure overdue positions are recognised and acted upon in a defined way.
items.applicability: Used when implementing overdue tracking, aging calculation, and collection-stage transitions.
items.example: An account moving past a configured days-past-due threshold is automatically advanced to the next collection stage and recorded with the trigger date.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Non-Performing Exposure Concept Awareness
items.description: Exposures that are significantly overdue or judged unlikely to be repaid in full are commonly treated as non-performing for monitoring and provisioning purposes. Systems should make the basis for such a classification explicit and reversible only through a recorded action. Awareness of these concepts helps avoid designs that classify or reclassify exposures without an auditable rationale.
items.applicability: Used when modelling exposure classification states and the events that change them.
items.example: An exposure flagged non-performing retains the trigger reason and date, and any return to performing status requires a recorded reassessment.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Insurance Supervision Awareness
items.description: Insurance activity in Mauritius falls under the non-bank financial services regulator, which oversees licensing and conduct in that sector. Middleware that interfaces with insurance products should respect that insurance is a distinct supervised domain with its own data and reporting expectations. Awareness of this boundary helps teams avoid conflating banking and insurance control assumptions.
items.applicability: Used when integrating or modelling data flows that touch insurance products alongside banking functions.
items.example: An integration treats insurance policy data as a separate regulated domain, keeping its access scope and reporting hooks distinct from deposit-account data.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Non-Bank Financial Services Awareness
items.description: A range of non-bank financial activities, such as leasing, securities, and certain global-business services, are supervised separately from banks in Mauritius. Systems serving these activities may face different licensing, conduct, and reporting expectations than core banking. Awareness of the non-bank perimeter helps teams scope controls to the correct regulatory domain.
items.applicability: Used when a middleware component serves activities that may sit under non-bank rather than banking supervision.
items.example: A module serving a leasing line records that it operates under non-bank financial-services assumptions, prompting a domain-appropriate control review.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Auditability Awareness
items.description: Financial systems are generally expected to be auditable, meaning significant actions can be reconstructed from durable records. Software should capture who did what, when, and on what basis for actions that affect money, customer data, or control state. Awareness of auditability supports the evidence-on-demand posture expected in a supervised estate.
items.applicability: Used when deciding which actions require durable, tamper-evident audit records.
items.example: Each approval, override, and status change writes an append-only audit entry that cannot be edited after the fact.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Segregation of Duties Awareness
items.description: Segregation of duties separates the ability to initiate, approve, and record an action so that no single individual controls an entire sensitive process. Systems should enforce role boundaries rather than rely on convention. Awareness of this principle helps prevent designs where one account can both create and approve the same transaction.
items.applicability: Used when assigning role permissions across initiation, approval, and recording functions.
items.example: The application rejects an approval where the approver identity equals the initiator identity for the same item.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Maker/Checker/Approver Control Awareness
items.description: The maker/checker/approver pattern routes a sensitive action through distinct preparation, verification, and authorisation roles. Systems should model these stages explicitly so that each step is recorded and cannot be skipped. Awareness of this pattern helps ensure high-impact actions carry independent review before they take effect.
items.applicability: Used when implementing multi-stage authorisation for high-impact operations.
items.example: A limit change is prepared by a maker, verified by a checker, and authorised by an approver, with all three identities and timestamps stored before it applies.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Banking Operations Exception Handling Awareness
items.description: Operational exceptions, such as failed transfers or rejected items, should be captured into a controlled queue rather than discarded or silently retried. Systems should make exceptions visible, attributable, and resolvable with a recorded outcome. Awareness of exception discipline reduces the chance that failures are lost or resolved without trace.
items.applicability: Used when designing failure paths in transaction-processing pipelines.
items.example: A failed item is routed to an exception queue with its error reason, and resolution requires an operator action that records the outcome.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Operational Risk Awareness
items.description: Operational risk covers loss arising from inadequate or failed processes, people, systems, or external events. Software design choices that improve traceability, controlled failure handling, and resilience directly support operational-risk management. Awareness of this lens helps teams weigh reliability and control alongside functionality.
items.applicability: Used when assessing the resilience and control posture of a processing component.
items.example: A batch process records partial completion and resumes safely after interruption instead of reprocessing from the start and risking duplicates.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Compliance Evidence Awareness
items.description: Demonstrating that a control operated is usually as important as the control existing, which makes evidence capture a first-class concern. Systems should produce durable artefacts that show a control ran, with inputs, outcome, and timing. Awareness of evidence needs helps teams design controls that are demonstrable rather than merely present.
items.applicability: Used when designing controls whose operation may need to be evidenced during review.
items.example: A daily sanctions-screening job writes a run record with the dataset version, item count, and outcome summary that can be retrieved later.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Control Testing Awareness
items.description: Controls are periodically tested to confirm they operate as intended, and software can make such testing easier or harder by how it exposes control state. Systems should provide deterministic, inspectable behaviour so that a tester can confirm a control triggers under defined conditions. Awareness of control testing supports designs that are verifiable rather than opaque.
items.applicability: Used when building controls that will need to be sampled or tested for effectiveness.
items.example: A control exposes a test-friendly outcome record so a reviewer can confirm that a deliberately non-compliant input was correctly blocked.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Regulatory Reporting Awareness
items.description: Institutions submit periodic returns and reports to supervisors, and the quality of those reports depends on the integrity of the underlying data. Systems should make the lineage from source data to a reported figure traceable so that reported numbers can be reconstructed and explained. Awareness of reporting themes helps teams avoid designs where report figures cannot be reconciled to source records.
items.applicability: Used when building data feeds or aggregations that contribute to regulatory or supervisory reports.
items.example: A reported aggregate stores the query parameters and as-of timestamp used to compute it, so the same figure can be regenerated and traced to source rows.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.

items.category: Mauritius Banking and Financial Services Context
items.title: Companies Act Record-Keeping Awareness
items.description: Corporate entities in Mauritius are subject to company-law obligations including maintaining accurate records and identifying beneficial ownership. Systems that capture corporate-customer data should support clear records of ownership and control structures. Awareness of these obligations complements due-diligence work on non-individual customers.
items.applicability: Used when modelling corporate-customer onboarding and beneficial-ownership data.
items.example: A corporate-customer record links to its directors and beneficial owners with effective dates, so ownership history is reconstructable.
items.limitation: Awareness only for the Mauritius context; not legal/regulatory advice and not a claim of Bank of Mauritius or FSC compliance. Evidence and current official guidance required.
